dataset: Dental CBCT DICOM (anonymized) with pre-placed implant

pi_items.source: Verified via env.json mount configuration: examples/blue_sky_plan_env/data is mounted read-only to /workspace/data (C:\workspace\data\ on Windows). setup_task.ps1 line 64 confirms this path and uses Launch-BlueSkyPlanWithFile to open it. NOTE: The .bsp file itself does not yet exist in the repository and must be manually created by importing the DICOM data into BSP and placing a deliberately flawed implant.

Verified via setup_task.ps1 lines 37-38: creates this directory with New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force. Also verified via live environment testing: the pre_task hook successfully creates this directory. export_result.ps1 reads output files from this exact path.
Consistent with task description ('reviewed_plan.bsp — the corrected project file') and export_result.ps1 line 14 ($bspFile variable). setup_task.ps1 cleans this file before each run (line 43).
Consistent with task description ('before_correction.png — cross-section at #30 before any changes') and export_result.ps1 line 15 ($beforeScreenshot variable).
Consistent with task description ('after_correction.png — cross-section at #30 after corrections') and export_result.ps1 line 16 ($afterScreenshot variable).

Consistent with task description ('qa_report.txt — document: original measurements...') and export_result.ps1 line 17 ($reportFile variable). Report content is read in full by export_result.ps1 for later verification.
Verified via export_result.ps1 line 18 ($resultFile) and line 224 (Out-File). Also confirmed export_result.ps1 writes a copy to C:\workspace\task_result.json (line 228) for verifier access.

The 2.0 mm safety distance from implant apex to the inferior alveolar nerve canal is the classical conservative standard established by Misch & Crawford (1990) and corroborated by Bartling et al. (1999). A 2024 systematic review and meta-analysis (PMC12221155, 1,636 patients) confirmed 0% neurosensory alteration rate at distances >= 2.0 mm. While current evidence suggests 1.0 mm may also be safe with precise CBCT imaging, 2.0 mm remains the widely recommended conservative threshold in clinical textbooks and guidelines. Verified via PubMed, Pocket Dentistry surgical complications reference, and Austin Publishing Group journal.

1.0 mm is the historical minimum floor for bone wall thickness, especially for lingual/palatal bone. Multiple references cite this as the absolute lower bound below which implant placement is contraindicated. The current ITI 7th Consensus Conference (2023, Monje et al., Clinical Oral Implants Research) recommends >= 1.5 mm buccally for optimal outcomes, but 1.0 mm remains valid as a hard minimum threshold in the literature. The task uses 1.0 mm as the safety floor, which is clinically defensible as a minimum standard. Verified via ITI consensus database, OsseoNews clinical forum, and Wiley/COIR publications.

Tooth #30 in the ADA Universal Numbering System is the lower right first molar. Confirmed by the American Dental Association Universal Tooth Designation System value set (ada.org), Radiopaedia (American Dental Association Universal Numbering System article), and DayoDental teeth chart. NOTE: The Universal Numbering System numbers 1-16 for the upper arch (right to left) and 17-32 for the lower arch (left to right). #30 = mandibular right first molar. IMPORTANT: A previous PI audit for complete_implant_workflow incorrectly marked this as 'contradicted' by confusing the ADA Universal system with the FDI (Federation Dentaire Internationale) system where tooth 30 would be in the lower left quadrant. Blue Sky Plan software uses the ADA Universal Numbering System, where #30 is definitively the lower right first molar.

Consistent with other blue_sky_plan_env tasks: complete_implant_workflow uses 70, implant_site_assessment uses 70, classify_bone_quality_misch uses 70. The verifier is currently a stub (returns score 100 always), so this threshold is used only by the VLM checklist evaluator. The value is reasonable for a multi-criterion scoring rubric.

privileged_info_summary: This is a dental implant quality assurance review task in Blue Sky Plan 5.0. The case contains a mandibular CBCT scan with a pre-placed implant at tooth #30 (lower right first molar per ADA Universal Numbering System, NOT FDI notation). The implant has been deliberately placed with safety violations that the agent must discover, correct, and document. Safety criteria: (1) minimum 2.0 mm clearance from implant apex to the inferior alveolar nerve canal (Misch & Crawford 1990 standard), (2) implant fully contained within bone with no buccal or lingual cortical plate perforation, (3) buccal and lingual bone walls each at least 1.0 mm thick. The deliberate violations in the pre-placed implant are: apex approximately 1.2-1.5 mm from the IAN (violating the 2.0 mm standard), implant positioned 1-1.5 mm toward the buccal plate (off-center, potentially violating the 1.0 mm buccal wall minimum), and slight lingual tilt of 8-12 degrees (causing the apical third to approach the lingual plate in adjacent cross-sections). These violations interact: moving the implant coronally to fix nerve clearance reduces bone engagement; moving it lingually to fix centering changes nerve clearance; correcting angulation changes both nerve clearance and wall thickness at every level. The agent must produce 4 output files in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\QAReview\: before_correction.png (annotated cross-section before changes), after_correction.png (annotated cross-section after changes), qa_report.txt (original measurements, violations found, corrections applied, final measurements), and reviewed_plan.bsp (the corrected project file). BSP project files (.bsp) are SQLite databases that can be queried for implant and measurement table data. Blue Sky Plan runs with Mesa LLVMpipe software OpenGL rendering (no GPU) via QT_OPENGL=software and MESA_GL_VERSION_OVERRIDE=4.5. The application is launched via schtasks /IT to run in the interactive desktop session. CRITICAL NOTE: The qa_review_case.bsp data file does not yet exist in the repository and must be manually created by importing the DICOM data (100 slices at examples/blue_sky_plan_env/data/dicom/) into BSP, placing an implant at tooth #30 with the described flaws, and saving the project.
